hawkish_phrases: inflation had increased to 3.4% in September and was expected to rise further in 2021
market-based measures and survey-based indicators of longer-term inflation expectations had moved closer to 2%
the longer the inflation spike lasted, the more it would become entrenched in longer-term inflation expectations
price pressures were more persistent than had been foreseen in the September ECB staff projections
upside risks to inflation had to be acknowledged
dovish_phrases: inflation was still expected to fall below the ECB's target in the medium term
net asset purchases under the PEPP at a moderately lower pace during the final quarter of the year
negotiated wage growth had remained subdued and there were no signs as yet of second-round effects on wages
inflation was expected to decline in the course of 2022
the accommodative monetary policy stance had to be reconfirmed and remained appropriate
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged and continued PEPP net purchases at a moderately lower pace in Q4 2021, acknowledging inflation had risen to 3.4% and would remain elevated longer than expected, but maintaining that inflation would decline in 2022 and settle below the 2% target over the medium term, with an overall accommodative stance bias.
